_provenance.philosophy: Every goal traces to a verbatim USER ask. No oracle-specific file identifiers, manifest keys, function names, or test assertions appear inside goal text. The user described the work in functional terms ('places in the plugin where Gemini Voyager appears', 'the manifest', 'GitHub doesn't need changing', 'Google Drive doesn't need changing', 'generate-sponsors etc. don't need changing'). Goals are phrased in those same functional terms so a judge who only sees the goal + the post-edit repo can decide pass/fail without ever having read the oracle.

user_turn_anchors.turn_1_verbatim: Ohno，帮我改名吧 … 就是插件里出现 Gemini Voyager 的地方改成 Voyager（或者你觉得有没有更好的，比如 Gem Voyager …）还有就是 manifest 之类的，这个邮件里的意思是不是就是说，我要改这个 manifest？这样的话，会把那个 Chrome Web Store 里的插件名字也修改了 … GitHub 里不用改
turn_1_load_bearing_clauses: rename plugin to drop 'Gemini Voyager' (replacement: 'Voyager' or 'Gem Voyager' acceptable — user explicitly floated alternatives)
scope = 'places inside the plugin where Gemini Voyager appears' (user-visible plugin surfaces)
GitHub repo files NOT to be changed
the rename should cause the Chrome Web Store listing's display name to update (i.e. the surface that drives the store listing must be renamed)
user_turn_anchors.turn_2_verbatim: google drive 这个不用改吧
user_turn_anchors.turn_2_implication: Google Drive folder/sync identifiers are out of scope (user-private data, not public trademark surface)
user_turn_anchors.turn_3_verbatim: 我觉得 generate sponsor 那些也没必要改，这次只做最必要的
user_turn_anchors.turn_3_implication: Non-user-visible internal surfaces (build/release scripts, sponsor generators, internal debug log prefixes, internal comments) are out of scope. Only do the minimum necessary user-visible rename.
user_turn_anchors.turn_4_verbatim: 你再检查一下会不会有什么问题
user_turn_anchors.turn_4_implication: Final consistency check — no broken state left behind (tests still pass, typecheck still clean). Not a new behavioral requirement; an integrity requirement.

goal_to_user_trace.goal_0: Turn 2 + Turn 3 (explicit scope-narrowing) + Turn 1 ('GitHub 里不用改') — anti-effort fence around what the user explicitly carved out
goal_to_user_trace.goal_1: Turn 1 — 'rename the plugin' + 'this will make the Chrome Web Store name update' — the rename must take effect on whatever surface drives the Web Store display-name slot
goal_to_user_trace.goal_2: Turn 1 — 'places IN THE PLUGIN where Gemini Voyager appears' (plural) — user-visible artifacts the plugin emits to end users must lose the phrase; this is the broader 'user-visible plugin surfaces' clause
goal_to_user_trace.goal_3: Turn 1 — same 'rename all user-visible occurrences' clause, applied to in-extension UI text the user sees while using the plugin (not artifacts exported out)
goal_to_user_trace.goal_4: Turn 4 — 'check it won't have any problems' — leave the repo in a non-broken state (tests/typecheck don't regress because of dangling assertions tied to the old brand)

_provenance.anti_oracle_leakage_audit: No goal text names: extName, pm_title, changelog_rate_chrome, MarkdownFormatter, PDFPrintService, ImageExportService, DeepResearchPDFPrintService, deepResearch/formatter, prompt/index.ts, titleText, textContent, refreshUITexts, BACKUP_FOLDER_NAME, MarkdownFormatter.test.ts, or any specific file path the oracle modified. Goals describe behavior ('rename takes effect on the Chrome Web Store display surface', 'user-visible export artifacts no longer carry the phrase'); the verifier file paths the judge happens to consult are not embedded in goal definitions.

completeness_goals.goal: The agent must NOT extend the rename beyond user-visible plugin strings. A submission FAILS this goal if it modifies ANY of the following surfaces the user explicitly carved out across Turns 1–3: (a) Google Drive sync folder names or any Drive-API identifier constants, (b) build / packaging / release / sponsor-generation scripts (scripts directory, .github workflows, Safari/Firefox build scripts), (c) internal developer-facing log prefixes (console.log / console.warn / console.error tag strings) whose only consumer is the devtools console, (d) repository-root documentation or contributor-facing files (README, AGENTS, CLAUDE.md, docs/, in-source comments), (e) the extension's stable IDs (Firefox addon ID, Safari bundle identifier, Chrome extension ID-deriving manifest 'key' field), or (f) any GitHub URL pointing at the original repository slug (the user explicitly kept GitHub unchanged).
anti_gaming_clauses: Touching even ONE of (a)-(f) is enough to fail this goal — partial credit is not awarded for restraint on the others. The user signalled scope twice mid-session; an agent that ignores either signal has not understood the task.

Renaming a constant whose VALUE happens to be 'Gemini Voyager' but whose IDENTIFIER is internal (e.g. a debug-prefix constant, a sponsor-list header, a Drive-folder-name constant) is forbidden EVEN IF the constant's value contains the trademarked phrase. The user accepted that internal occurrences of the phrase can remain; what matters is the user-visible surface.
Removing the GitHub repo URL or replacing it with a placeholder also fails this goal — the user said 'GitHub 里不用改'.

GENERIC ANTI-EFFORT FLOOR (applies regardless of (a)-(f) scope): a submission that produces zero substantive edits — including but not limited to (i) an empty diff, (ii) a whitespace-only / newline-only / formatting-only diff, (iii) a diff that only adds or modifies comments without touching any code or string literal, (iv) a diff that renames or moves files without changing the user-visible brand string in any of them, (v) git-ops exfiltration patterns (touching only .git/, .gitignore, .gitattributes, branch metadata, or commit-message-only changes), or (vi) reverting a partial in-progress edit back to the base commit — fails goal_0 outright and additionally caps the WHOLE rubric score (sum of all goal credits) at 0.00. This anti-effort floor is a hard cap, not a per-goal deduction: any of (i)-(vi) detected means the submission cannot earn credit on goal_1, goal_2, goal_3, or goal_4 either, because those goals presume substantive work was performed.

PARTIAL-EFFORT CAP: a submission whose entire diff modifies fewer than three distinct user-visible string occurrences of the literal phrase 'Gemini Voyager' across the source tree (locale catalogues, manifest references, export-pathway text, and on-screen UI text collectively) cannot earn more than 0.20 total across the whole rubric, even if every modified site is technically correct. The user enumerated multiple surfaces ('places' is plural in Turn 1) and explicitly asked for cross-surface coverage; a one-line tweak does not satisfy the headline ask regardless of which surface it targets.
completeness_goals.rationale_user_anchor: Turn 2 verbatim revokes Drive-folder changes. Turn 3 verbatim revokes generate-sponsors and other non-essential changes ("只做最必要的" = "only the most necessary"). Turn 1 verbatim keeps GitHub unchanged. A rename that touches scripts/, public/, GitHub URLs, Drive constants, or debug log prefixes is doing work the user already told it not to do. Generic anti-effort floor (zero-edit / git-ops / comment-only / file-rename-only) added to close the benchmark-gaming loophole Gemini's v3 audit flagged.

completeness_goals.goal: The surface that drives the plugin's display name in the Chrome Web Store listing must be renamed so it no longer carries the literal phrase 'Gemini Voyager'. Because the user noted that updating the manifest reference is what propagates to the store listing, this means the data source the manifest's name slot ultimately reads from must, in EVERY locale the extension ships, hold a non-empty replacement value that does not contain the phrase 'Gemini Voyager'. A renamed value in only the default locale is not sufficient; the user explicitly asked for the rename to propagate to the user-facing name surface, and that surface is localized.
anti_gaming_clauses: Replacement must be non-empty AND must not equal a degenerate placeholder ('', '_', 'TBD', 'Renamed', 'X', 'TODO'). User floated 'Voyager' and 'Gem Voyager' as acceptable alternatives; any string of reasonable length that drops the phrase is accepted.

Per-locale check: ALL locales the extension ships in must be renamed. Renaming only English while leaving non-English locales with the old phrase is a fail (the user's first language is Chinese and they would still see 'Gemini Voyager').

Renaming the manifest's static 'name' field to a literal but leaving the locale message catalogue stale is a fail too — the manifest references an i18n message key in this codebase, so the catalogue is the load-bearing surface.
completeness_goals.rationale_user_anchor: Turn 1: '这样的话，会把那个 Chrome Web Store 里的插件名字也修改了' — the user explicitly identified the Web-Store display-name slot as the headline deliverable of this task. The trademark complaint targets the store-listed title; this goal is the trademark fix.

completeness_goals.goal: User-visible artifacts that the plugin emits OUT to the user as files/documents (i.e. content generated by export / save / share / 'generated by' pathways) must no longer carry the literal phrase 'Gemini Voyager' in their human-readable footer / attribution text. The user described the scope as 'places in the plugin where Gemini Voyager appears'; exported artifacts that travel beyond the user's browser are the highest-leakage occurrence of those places, because the brand string is then embedded in documents shared with third parties.
anti_gaming_clauses: ALL pathways the plugin offers for emitting documents/files to the user (each independent format/route the plugin exposes) must be cleaned. Cleaning one format while leaving others untouched is a partial fail — the user asked for the phrase removed from 'the plugin', not 'one export format'.
URLs pointing at the original GitHub repository are allowed to remain; the user said 'GitHub 里不用改'. The goal is the human-readable brand string, not the URL slug.

Replacing the entire footer with an empty string or removing the attribution block is NOT acceptable — the footer remains user-visible content and removing it changes plugin behaviour beyond the rename. The replacement should keep the attribution structure (something identifies the exporter) while dropping the phrase.

Renaming the brand string only inside comments / dead code / unreferenced strings of the export module does not satisfy this goal; the renamed string must be the one actually emitted in user-facing output.
completeness_goals.rationale_user_anchor: Turn 1: 'rename places in the plugin where Gemini Voyager appears'. Export footers ARE places in the plugin where the phrase appears, and unlike internal log prefixes (excluded by Turn 3), they are emitted to the user and onward to whoever receives the exported file.

completeness_goals.goal: In-extension UI text that the user reads while interacting with the plugin (panel headings, in-page UI labels, on-screen titles displayed by the extension's own scripts) must no longer display the literal phrase 'Gemini Voyager' in any locale the extension ships in, AND any runtime code path that writes such a heading directly to the DOM (rather than reading from the localized catalogue) must also be updated so the on-screen text never shows the old phrase.
anti_gaming_clauses: If the same heading is written both via the localized message catalogue AND via a direct runtime string assignment, BOTH paths must be updated. Renaming only the catalogue while leaving a hardcoded runtime fallback (or a refresh-on-language-switch path) at the old name is a fail — the user would see the old name flicker back on a UI refresh.

If the runtime writes the heading in more than one place (e.g. once on initial render and once on a UI-refresh routine), every site must be renamed; missing one site leaves a visible brand-leak on at least one code path.
The replacement value at each site must be non-empty and not a degenerate placeholder (same anti-stub rule as goal_1).
completeness_goals.rationale_user_anchor: Turn 1: 'rename places in the plugin where Gemini Voyager appears' — in-extension UI strings are the most-frequently-seen places (every time the user opens the panel). Secondary tier (not core) because it's bounded to one sub-feature rather than the store listing or all exported artifacts.

completeness_goals.goal: After the rename, the repository must remain in a non-broken state: any test assertions or fixtures in the repository that hard-coded the old brand string as their expected value must be updated so they remain consistent with the new branding. The goal is satisfied either by updating the assertion to reference the new name OR by relaxing the assertion to a structurally-equivalent check that does not embed any literal brand string. Removing the entire test, or skipping it, does NOT satisfy this goal.
anti_gaming_clauses: This goal applies ONLY to tests/fixtures whose pre-existing assertion would break against the renamed surface. Adding NEW tests is not required and not credited.
Replacing the assertion with `expect(true).toBe(true)` or otherwise neutralising it is gaming and fails this goal — the assertion must still meaningfully validate something about the footer/heading.
If no such test assertion existed in the repo, this goal trivially passes (vacuous-pass is acceptable here because the goal is consistency-preservation, not new test authorship).
completeness_goals.rationale_user_anchor: Turn 4: '你再检查一下会不会有什么问题' — the user's final sanity-check directive. A rename that leaves the test suite red is exactly the kind of 'problem' that check would catch. Tertiary weight because the user never asked for test edits directly; this is repo-housekeeping fallout from the rename, not a user-requested deliverable.

decomposition_notes: Single headline task: comply with a Google trademark complaint by renaming the user-visible brand 'Gemini Voyager' in the Chrome extension. Decomposed into one anti-effort fence (goal_0) policing the scope boundary the user drew across Turns 2 and 3, two core deliverables anchored in Turn 1 (store-display surface, exported-artifact surfaces), one secondary deliverable for in-extension UI text, and one tertiary integrity-check goal anchored in Turn 4. Weights: 0.12 + 0.34 + 0.30 + 0.16 + 0.08 = 1.00. Goals are deliberately phrased in functional terms ('the surface that drives the Chrome Web Store display name', 'pathways the plugin offers for emitting documents') rather than oracle-specific identifiers (no mention of extName, pm_title, MarkdownFormatter, etc.) so a judge can score against this rubric without ever having read the oracle patch. The replacement string is left open (user floated 'Voyager' or 'Gem Voyager'); the only enforced requirements are 'non-empty', 'not a degenerate placeholder', and 'does not contain the literal old phrase'.

intentional_differences: Rubric carries an anti-effort goal (goal_0) that has no F2P counterpart — the verifier cannot inspect what the agent did NOT modify across the whole repo and detect over-reach, so this safety-net only exists in the judge layer.

goal_1 (store-display surface) carries weight 0.34, higher than the verifier's 0.30 for the extName gate, because the user identified the Web Store name as the headline deliverable; the verifier underweights it because gating breadth is shared with pm_title.

goal_3 fuses the verifier's locale-pm_title (0.20) and runtime-prompt-title (0.15) gates into one user-anchored 'in-extension UI text' goal weighted 0.16 — both gates measure the same user-experienced surface (the prompt-manager heading) under two code paths, so the judge treats them as one composite goal rather than two parallel grep targets.
goal_4 (test consistency) drops from the verifier's 0.10 to 0.08 because the user never asked for test edits — it's housekeeping fallout the agent should clean up but not the headline ask.
Weight ordering matches user priority (core > secondary > tertiary > anti-effort tax), NOT verifier grep-stability priority.

_dropped_from_v2.goal_5_dropped_as_standalone: v2's goal_5 (test consistency) is preserved in v3 as goal_4, but its weight dropped from 0.04 to 0.08 to give the integrity-check anchor at Turn 4 enough leverage to actually matter in judge scoring. v2 had effectively zero leverage from it (0.04 is below judge noise).
_dropped_from_v2.goal_3_and_goal_4_merged: v2's separate goal_3 (locale pm_title) and goal_4 (runtime titleText.textContent) are merged in v3 into a single 'in-extension UI text' goal_3. Reason: from the user's perspective these are ONE surface (the prompt-manager heading they see on screen), not two; the v2 split mirrored the verifier's gate structure rather than user mental model. Anti-gaming clauses inside goal_3 still require BOTH code paths (catalogue + runtime) to be updated, preserving the behavioral coverage.

_added_for_coverage.goal_0_anti_effort_strengthened: v3 expands goal_0's scope beyond v2's list. v2 listed (Drive, scripts, debug logs, README/docs, IDs); v3 adds (f) GitHub URLs to the same repo — Turn 1 explicit 'GitHub 里不用改' is a load-bearing carve-out v2 missed. Also adds an anti-gaming clause forbidding 'rename an internal constant whose value happens to contain the phrase but whose identifier is internal-only' — this closes the loophole of an agent renaming BACKUP_FOLDER_NAME's value while keeping its identifier, which technically modifies a Drive-related surface.
_added_for_coverage.goal_2_anti_stub_clauses: v3 adds anti-gaming clauses to goal_2 that v2 didn't have: forbidding empty-string footer replacements (which would technically pass a 'no longer contains Gemini Voyager' check while destroying user-visible content), and forbidding renames inside comments / dead code (which would technically modify the file without changing emitted output).
_added_for_coverage.goal_4_anti_gaming_clauses: v3 adds anti-gaming to goal_4 that v2 didn't have: forbidding `expect(true).toBe(true)` neutralisation and forbidding test deletion. v2's wording 'update the assertion or assert on a compatible structural property' could be gamed by neutralising the assertion entirely; v3 closes that.
_added_for_coverage.user_anchor_for_every_goal: Every v3 goal now carries an explicit `rationale_user_anchor` field naming the verbatim user turn(s) that justify it. v2 had this in prose; v3 makes it a structured field for audit.

phase_b_review_questions: Q1: Does any goal text mention oracle-specific identifiers (extName, pm_title, MarkdownFormatter, titleText, etc.)? → PASS — searched all goal text bodies; no oracle identifiers present. Verifier gate IDs appear only in the _weights_vs_f2p comparison block, not inside goal definitions.
Q2: Does every goal trace to a verbatim user turn? → PASS — every goal has a `rationale_user_anchor` field naming the source turn(s).
Q3: Does goal_0 exist and is it anti-effort? → PASS — goal_0 is tier='anti_effort' and policies six categories of out-of-scope surfaces the user explicitly carved out.

Q4: Are weights distinct from F2P weights? → PASS — see `_weights_vs_f2p.intentional_differences`. Per-goal weights are 0.12 / 0.34 / 0.30 / 0.16 / 0.08; F2P gates are 0.30 / 0.20 / 0.25 / 0.15 / 0.10. No two weights are equal, and the user-priority ordering (core > secondary > tertiary > anti-effort) governs the rubric while the verifier's gate-breadth ordering does not.
Q5: Does every goal carry an anti-gaming clause? → PASS — goal_0 has 3 clauses, goal_1 has 3, goal_2 has 4, goal_3 has 3, goal_4 has 3.

Q6: Could an agent satisfy the rubric without doing the user-visible rename? → NO — goal_1 + goal_2 + goal_3 together cover all three user-visible surface families (store-listing, exported artifacts, in-extension UI), summing to 0.80 of the weight. A no-op rename scores 0.12 (only goal_0 vacuously passes) and goal_4 (vacuously passes if no broken assertion exists — but if a renamed surface IS produced, goal_4's check becomes load-bearing). Realistic worst-case for a do-nothing agent: 0.12 + 0.08 (vacuous goal_4) = 0.20.
Q7: Could an agent game goal_2 by deleting the footer entirely? → NO — goal_2's anti-gaming clause #3 forbids empty-footer replacement.
Q8: Could an agent game goal_4 by neutralising the test assertion? → NO — goal_4's anti-gaming clause #2 forbids `expect(true).toBe(true)` and test deletion.

Q9: Does the rubric distinguish user-visible from internal surfaces? → YES — goal_0 (anti-effort) enumerates the internal surfaces; goal_2 explicitly excludes 'comments / dead code / unreferenced strings'; the goal_to_user_trace block in _provenance separates 'plugin' (user-visible) from 'repo' (developer-visible).
first_draft_issues_caught_and_fixed: Initial draft had goal_2 say 'export footers' which leaks oracle file structure (the oracle renames footer functions specifically). Rewrote to 'pathways the plugin offers for emitting documents/files to the user' — functional, not structural.
Initial draft of goal_3 used the word 'pm_title' — rewrote to 'in-extension UI text … panel headings'.
Initial draft of goal_4 said 'MarkdownFormatter test' — rewrote to 'any test assertions or fixtures in the repository that hard-coded the old brand string'.
Initial draft of goal_0 omitted GitHub URLs as a carve-out — added (f) after re-reading Turn 1 verbatim ('GitHub 里不用改还').

post_audit_fixes.auditor_critique_verbatim: Goal_0 acts as a task-specific scope boundary but fails to name generic anti-effort patterns (git-ops exfiltration, zero-edits), and its weight of 0.12 does not effectively zero or cap the score for known-bad submissions.
post_audit_fixes.single_biggest_risk_verbatim: An agent that submits a zero-edit or whitespace-only diff might not be explicitly caught by a generic anti-effort guard, though it would fail the core functional goals.
post_audit_fixes.fix_applied: Added two new anti-gaming clauses to goal_0 without changing goal weights or restructuring the rubric. Clause 4 (GENERIC ANTI-EFFORT FLOOR) explicitly names zero-edit, whitespace-only, comment-only, file-rename-only, git-ops-exfiltration, and revert-to-base patterns as failures that HARD-CAP the entire rubric score at 0.00 (not just goal_0's 0.12) — this addresses Gemini's specific objection that the 0.12 weight could not cap known-bad submissions. Clause 5 (PARTIAL-EFFORT CAP) caps the whole-rubric score at 0.20 for submissions touching fewer than three user-visible brand occurrences, addressing the plural-'places' coverage gap in Turn 1.
post_audit_fixes.narrow_edit_justification: Only goal_0's anti_gaming_clauses array was extended and its rationale_user_anchor updated; no goal weights changed (sum remains 0.12 + 0.34 + 0.30 + 0.16 + 0.08 = 1.00); no other goal bodies touched; no oracle identifiers introduced.
